title: Crux News — Revealed: The QLDC conversation Skyline's Richard Thomas tried to hide
articleTitle: Revealed: The QLDC conversation Skyline's Richard Thomas tried to hide
author: Peter Newport

noticePrinciples.summary: Both articles present editorial assertions and speculation as established facts, embed unproven claims within questions, and mischaracterise evidence in a manner that fails to draw a clear distinction between factual information and comment or opinion.

Both articles treat Mr Thomas unfairly by presenting his positions in a misleading light, omitting material context including the substance of the Council’s own findings, and relitigating upheld complaints through fresh unsubstantiated allegations.

title: Crux News — Lakeview construction starts - but major financial questions remain
articleTitle: Lakeview construction starts - but major financial questions remain
author: Peter Newport

noticePrinciples.summary: The article fails to distinguish between factual information and editorial opinion. Disputed financial figures are presented as established facts. Editorial characterisations of named individuals, speculative conclusions about the project's financial outcomes, rhetorical questions, and policy advocacy are embedded throughout an unlabelled news article. The article links to and relies upon a ten-part series exhibiting the same violations at scale, effectively importing those violations by reference.

title: Crux News — New security and cost questions around QLDC's CCTV contract
articleTitle: New security and cost questions around QLDC's CCTV contract
author: Peter Newport

noticePrinciples.summary: The article presents the author's inferences and a speculative cost extrapolation as factual findings in an unlabelled news report, and pursues two contradictory lines of criticism without acknowledging the inconsistency, reflecting editorial disposition rather than factual reporting.

The article links QLDC's cameras to a human rights scandal on the basis of an unverified visual identification; QLDC received less than one working day to respond to serious allegations before publication; and Cougar Security's named directors were photographed and their work criticised without any response being obtained.

title: Crux News — Crux under intense attack from three different sources
articleTitle: Crux under intense attack from three different sources
author: Peter Newport

noticePrinciples.summary: The article contains a specific, verifiable false statement of fact about a LinkedIn post and its comments, presents unsubstantiated allegations against named parties without right of reply, and makes a serious allegation of editorial corruption without evidence.
The article systematically presents personal inference, subjective experience, and unsourced assertion as reportable fact, without clear labelling or distinction from factual content.

title: Crux News — The Bendigo mine supporters: Inside their threats of violence, sabotage, bombs and even death
articleTitle: The Bendigo mine supporters: Inside their threats of violence, sabotage, bombs and even death
author: Peter Newport

noticePrinciples.summary: The article states as fact that the Mine Supporters Facebook page “is being investigated by police” without citing any police source. The only basis disclosed is that complaints were lodged, which is materially different from a police investigation. The headline attributes the actions of individual posters to all mine supporters, the article lacks balance by providing no voice to ordinary supporters, and Mr Woodward is treated unfairly through editorial devices including a deliberately selected photograph and caption unrelated to the article’s subject.

The article is published as a news report but contains unlabelled editorial commentary throughout, including the characterisation of the page as taking “social media toxicity to a new level,” the speculative assertion that the page “appears to encourage, or condone, violence,” and a photo caption that editorialises by associating Mr Woodward with an environmental disaster at an unrelated mine. The article fails to clearly distinguish between factual reporting and the author’s editorial commentary.

title: Crux News — Ombudsman investigation: QLDC refuses Cougar contract details
articleTitle: Ombudsman investigation: QLDC refuses Cougar contract details
author: Peter Newport
summary: Complaint concerning Crux News article about QLDC's responses to LGOIMA requests regarding Cougar Security contracts and CCTV cameras.

noticePrinciples.summary: The article presents editorial conclusions about QLDC's conduct and motivations as established facts, without labelling them as opinion or analysis. Phrases such as "basically — it's too hard," the scare-quoted "find," and the framing of the camera arrangement as a suspicious unanswered question are editorial judgements, not reported facts. No opinion or analysis label is applied anywhere in the article.

The article states that 447 CCTV cameras were "bought using ratepayer funds but given to Cougar." QLDC's own LGOIMA response — published by Crux as a source document — states that Cougar is contracted to maintain and operate QLDC's network and that no cameras have been transferred. The characterisation is inaccurate. QLDC was not given an opportunity to respond to this specific claim within the article.
